message:
fix(watch): use pane.ID for output capture and state tracking

Fixed pane identification in the watch command loop to use the pane's
actual ID field instead of manually constructing a "session:index"
string.

The previous approach had issues:
- Constructed paneID as fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", session, pane.Index)
- This assumed pane indices are stable and directly usable as tmux
  pane identifiers
- In practice, tmux pane IDs have a different format (%N) and the
  constructed string could fail to match actual panes

The fix:
- Use pane.ID directly for tmux.CapturePaneOutput calls
- Use pane.ID as the map key for paneStates tracking
- This ensures consistent identification across the polling loop

This resolves watch output capture failures that could occur when
sessions had complex pane arrangements or after panes were
added/removed.
